title: Configure post-hydration RPC path (browser → business-logic)
description: After hydration, the browser must talk directly to the business-logic server via connect-es — NOT through the rendering server. Configure the connect-es transport in the client to point at the business-logic server's published port. Verify mutations and refetches bypass the rendering server.

title: Implement client entry point with hydrate()
description: Create the client-side entry point that reads dehydrated TanStack Query state from the embedded script tag, rehydrates the QueryClient cache, and calls SolidJS hydrate() to attach reactivity to server-rendered DOM. After hydration, the app behaves as a SPA.

title: Integrate TanStack Query with dehydrate/hydrate flow
description: Set up solid-query (TanStack Query for SolidJS) with QueryClient. During SSR: create a QueryClient per request, run data loaders that populate the cache via connect-es RPC calls, dehydrate the cache, and embed the serialized state in the HTML as a script tag. On the client: read the embedded state and rehydrate into the client QueryClient before hydrate() runs.

title: Wire renderToStream SSR with manifest-driven preloads
description: Implement the SSR handler in Hono: invoke renderToStream from solid-js/web, call renderTags() from @solidjs/meta for head injection, read the Rsbuild manifest to inject modulepreload and stylesheet link tags for the matched route's chunks. Stream the response.

title: Create SolidJS app shell with router and meta
description: Build the SolidJS application root: App component wrapped in MetaProvider, @solidjs/router with route definitions, layout component. Configure isomorphic routing (url prop on server, browser location on client). Set up basic Title and Meta tags.

title: Scaffold web-ui-ssr server with Bun + Hono
description: Create the Bun + Hono HTTP server for the rendering service. Set up the entry point, Hono app, and static asset serving for client bundles. This server will handle SSR requests and serve the built client-side JS/CSS.

title: Implement connect-es RPC endpoints for TODO CRUD
description: Wire the generated connect-es service implementation into the Hono server. Implement ListTodos, GetTodo, CreateTodo, UpdateTodo, DeleteTodo handlers that delegate to the SQLite persistence layer. Enable CORS for browser-direct access after hydration.

title: Scaffold business-logic server with Bun + Hono
description: Create the Bun + Hono HTTP server for the business-logic service. Set up the entry point, basic Hono app, and health check endpoint. This server will host connect-es RPC endpoints.

title: Set up connect-es code generation and shared RPC client
description: Configure buf or protoc-gen-es to generate TypeScript connect-es client code from the proto definitions. The generated client must be importable from both the SSR server (node environment) and the browser (web environment) so the same typed RPC methods are used on both paths.

title: Define Todo service protobuf schema
description: Create .proto file(s) defining the Todo service contract: ListTodos, GetTodo, CreateTodo, UpdateTodo, DeleteTodo RPCs with their request/response message types. This is the shared contract used by both services.
close_reason: Proto schema defined at proto/todo/v1/todo.proto with all 5 RPCs and message types

title: Configure Rsbuild with web and node environments
description: Set up rsbuild.config.ts in the web-ui-ssr service with two environments: 'web' for the client bundle (browser hydration) and 'node' for the SSR server bundle (Bun). Configure vanilla-extract plugin for zero-runtime CSS extraction. The build should produce paired JS+CSS chunks for lazy() boundaries and a manifest for preload injection.

title: Initialize monorepo directory structure
description: Create the two-service directory layout: services/web-ui-ssr/ and services/business-logic/ with their own package.json files. Set up root package.json with workspaces. Initialize TypeScript configs (root tsconfig.json + per-service).
close_reason: Completed: Monorepo structure initialized with services/web-ui-ssr and services/business-logic directories, package.json files with workspaces configured, and TypeScript configs (root + per-service) created.

title: Epic: Client Hydration & Post-Hydration RPC
description: Client entry point calling hydrate(), TanStack Query cache rehydration from embedded state, and post-hydration RPC path where the browser talks directly to the business-logic server via connect-es.

title: Epic: SSR Rendering Server & SolidJS Wiring
description: Bun + Hono rendering server with manual SolidJS SSR via renderToStream, isomorphic routing, head management, lazy() code splitting, vanilla-extract styling, and TanStack Query dehydration. The core of the experiment.

title: Epic: Business Logic Server
description: Bun + Hono server exposing connect-es RPC endpoints for TODO CRUD, backed by SQLite. This is the data-owning service in the two-service split.

title: Epic: Protobuf & connect-es RPC Layer
description: Define the Todo service proto, generate connect-es client, and configure a shared RPC client usable from both the SSR server and the browser. This is the contract between services.

title: Epic: Project Scaffolding & Build Configuration
description: Set up the monorepo structure, TypeScript config, and Rsbuild with web+node environments. This is the foundation everything else builds on.

title: Run POC validation checklist
description: Verify all acceptance criteria from AGENTS.md: docker compose up works, streamed HTML contains TODO data, dehydrated TanStack Query state present, no refetch on hydration, post-hydration mutations go direct to business-logic, lazy() produces JS+CSS chunks, vanilla-extract in per-chunk CSS, head tags in initial HTML, SQLite persists across restarts. Document findings.

title: Implement TODO mutations (create, update, delete)
description: Wire up client-side mutations using TanStack Query's useMutation + connect-es RPC: CreateTodo, UpdateTodo (toggle complete), DeleteTodo. After hydration these go browser → business-logic directly. Invalidate/refetch the list query on success.

title: Build TODO item detail view (lazy-loaded route)
description: Implement a detail/edit route for a single TODO item, loaded via lazy() for code splitting. This route should produce a separate JS+CSS chunk pair. Uses GetTodo RPC in a Suspense-wrapped data loader. Demonstrates route-level splitting and vanilla-extract per-chunk CSS.

title: Build TODO list view (server-rendered)
description: Implement the main TODO list route component. It should use a TanStack Query data loader calling ListTodos RPC inside a Suspense boundary. The list renders server-side during SSR with real data from the business-logic server. Style with vanilla-extract.

title: Create docker-compose.yml with shared network and SQLite volume
description: Write docker-compose.yml wiring both services on a shared network. The business-logic server mounts a Docker volume for the SQLite file so data persists across down/up cycles. Both services expose their ports. The rendering server references the business-logic server by Compose service name.

title: Create Dockerfiles for both services
description: Write Dockerfiles for web-ui-ssr and business-logic services. Both use Bun as the runtime. The web-ui-ssr Dockerfile should include the Rsbuild build step. The business-logic Dockerfile just needs to run the Hono server.

title: Configure lazy() route splitting with vanilla-extract
description: Set up lazy() on route components so Rspack produces paired JS+CSS chunks per route. Ensure vanilla-extract styles are included in the per-chunk CSS extraction. Verify the manifest includes both JS and CSS assets for each lazy boundary so the server can inject correct preload tags.

title: Implement SQLite persistence layer
description: Set up SQLite database in the business-logic server for TODO persistence. Create the todos table schema and implement basic CRUD operations. Use bun:sqlite or better-sqlite3. The DB file path should be configurable via env var for Docker volume mounting.

title: Epic: TODO App Features & Validation
description: Build the actual TODO UI (list view, item view, mutations) and validate against the POC acceptance checklist. This is the deliverable that exercises all the moving parts.

title: Epic: Docker Compose Orchestration
description: Docker Compose setup wiring both services on a shared network, with SQLite volume mount for persistence across restarts. Single docker compose up to bring the POC online.
